summary: Generalized and improved tools/table-typer, then applied it to StageCallbacks and related table-derived callback signatures. The main decomp-facing result was changing StageCallbacks and many stage callback functions from generic HSD_GObj* parameters to Ground_GObj*, with necessary forward includes in affected headers. The tool also gained additional table type definitions, a fixtab subcommand, an unk subcommand for finding UNK_RET functions referenced by asm tables, better asm object parsing, and documentation. A decomp.dev bot report showed no GALE01 binary changes.
what_changed: Changed struct StageCallbacks in src/melee/gr/types.h so callback0, callback1, callback2, and callback3 take Ground_GObj* instead of HSD_GObj*.

Updated many stage implementation prototypes and definitions to match the new StageCallbacks function pointer types, replacing HSD_GObj* with Ground_GObj* in files such as grbattle.c, grfigure1.c, grfigure2.c, grfigure3.c, grgreatbay.c, gricemt.c, and many other gr*.c/gr*.h files.
Added gr/forward.h includes where public headers needed the Ground_GObj forward declaration, for example grbattle.h.

Extended table-typer's tableTypes map beyond the previous hard-coded ItemStateTable workflow: evidence in c.go shows ItemStateTable, ItemLogicTable, sdata_ItemGXLink, and the new StageCallbacks entry.
Renamed TableType.NumEntries to NumAsmEntries and modeled StageCallbacks as five asm entries: four function pointers plus a non-function u32/flags field.

Reworked table-typer CLI from positional arguments into subcommands using lukechampine.com/flagg: fixtab -type=... for applying type fixes, and unk for finding UNK_RET functions referenced from asm data tables.
Changed table-typer source/asm discovery to use a root directory and default paths under src/melee and build/GALE01/asm/melee.

Improved function signature parsing: preserves unnamed parameters in declarations, avoids treating lines with return as declarations, and assigns generated parameter names like gobj/gobj2 for definitions when needed.
Changed asm table extraction to split on .obj records and read byte/2byte/4byte/8byte entries, instead of the older section/global-oriented scan.
Updated README usage and known limitations, including documenting that the tool cannot automatically add missing #include statements.
Some item-side signatures were also adjusted from table-derived evidence, for example Item_GObj* parameters in itarwinglaser.c and int-to-bool corrections in item headers such as itbombhei.h.
Adjusted config/GALE01/symbols.txt around it_803F6CA8 by shrinking that object size from 0x158 to 0xB0 and adding lbl_803F6D58 at 0x803F6D58 with size 0xA8.
smart_moves: Used the asm table layout as the source of truth for callback signatures, allowing broad, consistent propagation of Ground_GObj* across stage callback functions instead of hand-editing each function independently.
Represented non-function table fields as nil entries in tableTypes, which let the tool skip StageCallbacks.flags while typing the function-pointer fields.
Added an unk subcommand to quantify and locate remaining UNK_RET functions referenced from asm tables; the PR body reports the count dropped from 123 to 119 after the StageCallbacks fixes.
Made table-typer support both headers and C files when locating table declarations, which matters because not all typed tables are necessarily declared only as externs in headers.
Kept the matching risk low: the decomp.dev GALE01 report said 'No changes', indicating the type/header/tooling edits preserved binary output.
Documented a known limitation instead of hiding it: table-typer rewrites signatures but cannot add all required #include statements automatically.
decomp_lessons: For callback tables, type the struct's function pointers first, then use tool-assisted propagation to bring all referenced function declarations/definitions into agreement.
Ground stage callbacks should use Ground_GObj* when they are invoked through StageCallbacks; retaining generic HSD_GObj* hides useful type information and leaves table signatures less precise.
When changing public prototypes from HSD_GObj* to domain-specific typedefs, add the correct forward header, such as gr/forward.h, rather than relying on baselib forward declarations.
Automated signature rewriting can be match-safe if it only changes C types/prototypes and the decomp.dev binary diff stays empty.
Do not assume every asm table entry is a function pointer; table typing needs explicit field metadata so scalar/flags fields are skipped.
Finding UNK_RET functions by scanning asm table references is a useful prioritization tactic because table layout often reveals return and parameter types.
naming_conventions: Typed ground object parameters use Ground_GObj* rather than HSD_GObj* for StageCallbacks targets.
Typed item object parameters use Item_GObj* for item table callbacks.
Generated or preserved parameter names in rewritten function definitions favor gobj for a single object parameter and gobj2 for a second generated object parameter when needed.
The table-typer command names introduced here are fixtab for applying table-based signature fixes and unk for finding UNK_RET table references.
StageCallbacks fields remain named callback0, callback1, callback2, callback3, and flags; the PR did not rename those fields.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The table-typer tool extracts asm data tables by parsing .obj blocks and collecting .byte, .2byte, .4byte, and .8byte entries.
For a known table type, table-typer maps each asm table entry position to a C function type and rewrites matching source/header signatures.
StageCallbacks was modeled as five asm entries: void Ground_GObj* callback, bool Ground_GObj* callback, void Ground_GObj* callback, void Ground_GObj* callback, then a skipped u32 flags field.
The unk command builds a set of symbols referenced by asm tables and searches C/H sources for UNK_RET declarations of those symbols.
The bot report for GALE01 stated 'No changes', supporting that the type propagation was non-codegen-changing.
The symbols.txt split near it_803F6CA8 corrected object boundaries in .data, reducing it_803F6CA8 size and naming a following lbl_803F6D58 object.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported: 'No changes' for GALE01, which is positive evidence that the PR preserved matching.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that run table-typer fixtab for ItemLogicTable or sdata_ItemGXLink.
Search for remaining UNK_RET functions referenced from asm tables after PR 1796.
Audit StageCallbacks users that still take HSD_GObj* instead of Ground_GObj* after this merge.
Look for follow-up fixes adding missing includes caused by table-typer rewrites.
Review object-boundary corrections around config/GALE01/symbols.txt it_803F6CA8 and lbl_803F6D58.
